name: Inspiratory pressure setting (Pinsp)
description: Set inspiratory pressure on the ventilator (pressure-controlled breaths), in cmH2O; a setting, not a measured pressure.

en.name: Inspiratory pressure setting (Pinsp)
en.shortDescription: Set inspiratory pressure on the ventilator (pressure-controlled breaths), in cmH2O; a setting, not a measured pressure.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

The inspiratory pressure setting is the airway pressure the clinician sets on the ventilator to deliver each breath in a pressure-controlled mode — the target pressure the ventilator applies during inspiration. In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, a ventilator setting rather than a measured airway pressure. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. It is reported in centimetres of water (**cmH2O**); some ventilators display it in millibar (mbar), which is nearly equivalent (1 cmH2O ≈ 0.98 mbar).

## Included Concepts

This set contains the single SNOMED concept for the inspiratory pressure setting:

- *4215838 / 417071008 — Inspiratory pressure setting (SNOMED)*: the set inspiratory pressure on the ventilator.

**Note on units**: reported in **`44777590 / cm[H2O] — centimeter watercolumn`** (UCUM), the recommended unit; a cmH2O↔mbar conversion is registered (1 cmH2O = 0.98 mbar).

## Mapping Notes

- Map the set inspiratory pressure on a pressure-controlled ventilator to *4215838 / 417071008 — Inspiratory pressure setting*. Convert a value in mbar to cmH2O (×1.02) before mapping.
- **Setting, not a measured pressure**: this is the *set* target pressure, distinct from measured airway pressures. Peak inspiratory pressure, plateau pressure, mean airway pressure and PEEP are measured/derived pressures captured in their own concept sets.
- **Whether this is the pressure above PEEP or the absolute inspiratory pressure depends on the ventilator** and is not distinguished by this concept; preserve any such detail in the source value if needed, but map the value here regardless.
- **Pressure support level** (the inspiratory assist set in a spontaneous/support mode) is a distinct ventilator setting and is not part of this concept set.

fr.name: Réglage de la pression inspiratoire (Pinsp)
fr.shortDescription: Pression inspiratoire réglée sur le ventilateur (cycles en pression contrôlée), en cmH2O ; un réglage, pas une pression mesurée.
